subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Construct and interpret complex frequency distributions
Draw histograms, cumulative frequency graphs (less than/greater than type), and frequency polygons
Compute mean, median, and mode using different methods for grouped and ungrouped data
Apply statistical measures to solve real-life problems
Analyze data patterns and draw conclusions

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Data Grouping
student_activities: Group raw data into appropriate classes
Construct frequency tables with unequal intervals

lesson_plan.title: Histograms and Frequency Polygons
topics_covered: Constructing histograms for unequal class widths
student_activities: Draw histograms and frequency polygons from complex tables

lesson_plan.title: Cumulative Frequency Graphs – Ogive
topics_covered: Less than and greater than cumulative frequency
Reading median, quartiles, and percentiles from ogives
teaching_strategy: Explain plotting of both types of cumulative graphs
student_activities: Plot less than and greater than ogives
Find median and quartiles from graphs

lesson_plan.title: Mean of Grouped Data – Assumed and Step Deviation Method
topics_covered: Step deviation method for large numbers
teaching_strategy: Solve examples on board with large data sets
student_activities: Compute mean using all three methods

lesson_plan.title: Median and Mode – Advanced Problems
topics_covered: Mode using formula for grouped data
student_activities: Find median and mode from complex frequency tables
Analyze data patterns and write conclusions

lesson_plan.title: Chapter Revision & Application-based Problems
topics_covered: Application to population, economics, and exam scores
student_activities: Solve mixed questions from past papers
Draw conclusions and discuss anomalies in data

teacher_tip: Emphasize the difference between less than and greater than ogives and encourage students to choose the correct method for mean, median, and mode based on data complexity.
